title: [Moore] Add %m/%M hierarchical path format specifier
body:
Add the `moore.fmt.hier_path` and `sim.fmt.hier_path` ops that can be used to print the absolute hierarchical path to the surrounding module instance. This corresponds to the `%m` and `%M` format specifiers in SystemVerilog. The actual path to be printed is defined implicitly by the parent op of the operation.

To actually support these operations in Arcilator, we will have to thread a `dbg.scope` or a format literal with the hierarchical path prefix through any modules that contain a `sim.fmt.hier_path` op. This will allow us to dynamically build up the format fragment to be printed without having to monomorphize the module hierarchy. That's future work though.

Error diff on sv-tests (including swrite commit):
```
-120 error: unsupported expression: EmptyArgument
+118 error: unsupported expression: element select into string
 -13 error: unsupported format specifier `%m`
  -4 error: counter overflow at time %t", $time);
  +4 error: unsupported format specifier `%L`
  -3 error: unsupported system call `$swrite`
  +3 error: failed to legalize operation 'moore.fstring_to_string'
  +1 error: 'llhd.halt' op expects parent op to be one of 'llhd.process, llhd.final'
  +1 error: unsupported format specifier `%v`
  +1 error: unsupported system call `$timeformat`
 -12 total change
```
